summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; covers the 7 June cutoff -> early 10 June; 8/9 June runs empty). **THE HEADLINE MARKET FACT: BRENT DID NOT BID THROUGH THE MAXIMUM KINETIC ESCALATION OF THE POST-APRIL WAR. Brent $92.10 (+0.7% 1D, -11.6% 1M, +4.9% 3M); the war-risk complex (gold, Brent, VIX) absorbed state-on-state missile exchanges, a downed US aircraft, US strikes on Iran, and Iranian strikes on three US-allied capitals — and crude barely moved.** June 9 closed $91.08 (-0.4%, below Friday's $93.09) — Brent FELL during a state-on-state exchange + the US-Iran kinetic opening; June 10 traded +0.9% intraday to ~$92.29 on the IRGC base attacks — UNDER a 1% 'war bid' for a three-country strike on US installations. The regime read is decisive: there is NO war premium left to unwind — the ~$20-30 structural premium over the ~$73 Feb baseline that defined the cycle has compressed; the market is now pricing the FED PATH (a December hike repriced to ~70% odds on hot May jobs + oil-fed inflation -> DXY ~99.9, 10Y pinned at 4.52%) and China demand softness, genuinely TWO-SIDED at ~$92. CRITICAL COROLLARY: an UNCALIBRATED event (confirmed US deaths, Hormuz mining, a Beirut-scale strike on Tehran) would hit a market positioned for choreography — the convexity is now maximally asymmetric BECAUSE the premium is gone. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: 'Brent $100 sustained 3+ sessions' STAYS RESOLVED — no clean >=$100 close (peak ~$92.29 intraday). 'Brent drops below $85 sustained' STAYS watching — not crossed (low ~$91). NEITHER crossed; both stay watching. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **BRENT FELL ~2% TO ~$93 ON 5 JUNE ON WEAKER GLOBAL DEMAND (CHINESE CRUDE IMPORTS AT A ~10-YEAR LOW) + NO US-IRAN BREAKTHROUGH — YET STILL +~4% ON THE WEEK ON THE RENEWED GULF CLASHES; THE STRUCTURAL ~$20-30 WAR PREMIUM HELD.** Brent settled $93.09 (-2.0% 1D; WTI $90.54, -2.7%), weighed by WEAKER global demand — Chinese crude imports fell to a ~10-YEAR LOW on soft refinery activity — plus the lack of a US-Iran breakthrough; it nonetheless stayed +~4% on the WEEK because the renewed 5-6 June Gulf clashes (7-missile salvo at Kuwait/Bahrain, 4 drones toward Hormuz, US Goruk/Qeshm strike) kept a thin tail premium bid. Hormuz throughput did NOT change (~10 ships/day vs ~95 normal); the structural ~$20-30 war premium over the ~$73 Feb baseline is intact. The cross-current is the regime tell: oil is caught between a supply-tail (war) and demand-destruction (China/global slowdown). **The cleanest read of 5 June: oil DOWN + gold DOWN + VIX UP together = a US-MACRO/AI event, NOT a Middle-East event** — had this been a ME-shock, oil and gold would have risen and VIX with them. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 6)**: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' STAYS RESOLVED — no clean >=$100 close. Asymmetry stays violent: a non-calibrated casualty-producing act or an OPERATIONAL Hormuz closure gaps Brent toward $110+; a signed deal / fuller global-demand crack grinds it to the high-$80s; the ~$20-30 structural premium is the floor between. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE RE-ESCALATION LEG REVERSED — BRENT FELL ~1% BELOW $97 (SETTLING ~$95.20) ON DIPLOMACY HOPES + GULF DE-ESCALATION; THE THIN TAIL PREMIUM RE-PRICED OUT, THE ~$30 STRUCTURAL PREMIUM HELD.** Brent fell ~1% to below $97 (settling ~$95.20, +0.2% on the snapshot; WTI $92.90), weighed by hopes the US and Iran could still reach a deal and reopen Hormuz, the Lebanon-ceasefire-within-24h framing, and the ABSENCE of a new Iranian Gulf barrage on 4 June (intel A4). This partly UNWINDS the 1-3 June re-escalation bid. The structural read is unchanged: the ~$30 war premium over the ~$73 Feb baseline is intact; what moved is the THIN TAIL premium re-pricing the THREAT, since Hormuz throughput did NOT change (~10 ships/day vs ~95 normal, still effectively closed, intel A9). Paper-physical bifurcation INTACT: paper-Brent prices the diplomacy; physical refuses to unwind — war-risk insurance +340% since 28 Feb ($10-14M/transit), VLCC TD3C last hard print ~WS467 / ~$474K/day (~4x normal). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 4)**: 'Brent breaks $100 sustained 3+ sessions' STAYS RESOLVED — no clean >=$100 close. Asymmetry stays violent: a non-calibrated casualty-producing act or an OPERATIONAL Hormuz closure gaps Brent toward $110+; a signed deal grinds it to the high-$80s (the ~$30 structural premium is the floor either way). The ~$30 structural premium above the ~$73 Feb-28 baseline is intact; the tail premium has re-entered ~$5-8 over three sessions. Watch for 3 clean closes >=$100. Asymmetry stays violent: a NON-calibrated casualty-producing act (US/allied-military deaths) or an OPERATIONAL Hormuz closure gaps Brent toward $110+; a Lebanon-driven Iran re-engagement retraces the ~+$5-8 tail premium toward the high-$80s.
